title: Prevent page layout shift and scrollbar hiding when opening dropdown menus
description: Address feedback from avaliacao-interface.md (item 3): when Radix UI DropdownMenu or Popover components open, body scroll locking or portal overlay logic hides the browser scrollbar, causing a minor horizontal layout shift (elements jump slightly in width). Fix this by configuring Radix UI components (or setting modal={false} / preventScroll={false} / CSS scrollbar-gutter: stable / Radix scroll lock overrides) so that opening dropdown menus and popovers in the admin shell does not hide the page scrollbar or shift elements on screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Opening any DropdownMenu (header user menu, sidebar actions, tab bar menu, editor block handles) does not hide the browser scrollbar on document.body
The width of the header, sidebar, and main content area remains completely stable without horizontal jumping/shifting when dropdowns or popovers open
Clicking outside a dropdown, pressing Escape, or navigating with arrow keys continues to dismiss and interact with popovers as expected

steps.description: Identify Radix UI scrollbar lock behavior
steps.details: Analyze Radix UI DropdownMenu and Popover portal triggers causing document.body overflow: hidden or padding adjustments on trigger open.

steps.description: Configure Radix primitives or CSS rules to preserve scrollbar
steps.details: Set modal={false} on non-blocking dropdown menus or enforce CSS scrollbar-gutter: stable / body scroll lock overrides so the page scrollbar remains visible.
Open and close all dropdown menus while viewing a page with a visible scrollbar and verify that zero horizontal shift or scrollbar flickering occurs.

technicalNotes: Radix UI primitives default modal={true} which adds body scroll lock and padding adjustments. For dropdown menus that don't block the viewport, modal={false} or custom container configuration preserves scrollbar layout.
